vitals.terrain: mountainous alpine roads with four major passes
vitals.participation_trend: consistently sold out with lottery system; 4,000 participants from 41 nations

course_description.overview: Brutal 227 km loop starting and finishing in Sölden, crossing four high Alpine passes with total 5,500 m elevation gain, recognized as one of Europe's toughest one-day road cycling events since 1981.

key_segments.name: Kühtai Pass
key_segments.description: Climb to 2,020 m after descent to Oetz.

key_segments.name: Jaufenpass
key_segments.description: Ascent to 2,090 m from Brenner area.

key_segments.name: Timmelsjoch
key_segments.description: Final major climb to 2,509 m before return to Sölden.

key_segments.name: Full Route
key_segments.description: Sölden – Längenfeld – Umhausen – Oetz (820 m) – Kühtai (2,020 m) – Kematen – Völs – Innsbruck – Brenner (1,377 m) – Jaufenpass (2,090 m) – Timmelsjoch (2,509 m) – Sölden.

course_description.difficulty_narrative: Mythical challenge demanding extreme endurance, power, and mental fortitude; crosses four passes with relentless climbing totaling 5,500 m over 227 km, considered a bucket-list event for elite amateur cyclists.

logistics.getting_there: Fly into Innsbruck Airport (INN), then transfer by bus or car to Sölden (approx. 1 hour drive); event centered in Ötztal Valley with good road access.
logistics.accommodation: Numerous hotels in Sölden and Ötztal Valley; many offer Ötztaler packages from March 9-19, 2026; book early due to high demand.
logistics.registration_details: Lottery system: Online registration January 1-31, 2026; first draw mid-February 2026, second draw early April 2026; payment deadlines March 8 and April 13, 2026; max 4,000 spots; transfers April 27-May 18, 2026; cancellations until June 23, 2026.
logistics.bike_logistics: Standard road bike; transport via bike boxes or event services; test race nutrition and pacing as per training plans.
logistics.local_considerations: Start 06:30 AM August 30, 2026; high altitude (up to 2,509 m), variable mountain weather; prepare for cold descents and hot valleys.

final_verdict.one_liner: Europe's ultimate road climbing sufferfest – if you conquer Ötztaler, you've summited the Everest of amateur cycling.
final_verdict.who_is_it_for: Elite amateurs with FTP >250W, strong climbers seeking prestige; not for beginners.
final_verdict.is_it_worth_it: Yes, for those chasing iconic status and personal limits; lottery makes entry exclusive.
pro_tips: Train 24 weeks with base/HIT phases peaking late August.
Secure spot via January lottery; check spam for draw notification.
Acclimatize to altitude; test nutrition on long climbs.
Book Sölden accommodation early for packages.

videos.description:
Es ist soweit: Der Ötztaler Radmarathon LIVE!
Folge über 4000 Teilnehmer bei ihrem Traum, heute als Finisher über die Ziellinie zu rollen. Ötztaler Radmarathons.

videos.title: Ötztaler Radmarathon 2023: Rennbericht
videos.description:
Ganze 4.336 Starterinnen und Starter stürzten sich in den Traum des Ötztaler Radmarathons. Für 3.869 Teilnehmerinnen und Teilnehmer ist der Traum Wirklichkeit geworden und sie dürfen sich FINISHER des Ötztaler Radmarathons 2023 nennen! Wie weit haben euch eure Träume gebracht? Hört niemals auf zu träumen, denn wir sehen uns bald wieder! Lehnt euch zurück, entspannt euch und genießt unseren diesjährigen Rennbericht 2023!
